aws.guardduty.enabled.description: Check if AWS GuardDuty is enabled for security monitoring (CCCS CA-2 Control Assessments)
aws.guardduty.enabled.notes: enabled_in_all_regions covers the 'enabled' requirement across all regions

aws.guardduty.centrally_managed.description: Check if GuardDuty is centrally managed (likely via AWS Organizations)
aws.guardduty.centrally_managed.notes: No rule checks central management via Organizations - needs new implementation

aws.guardduty.eks_audit_log_enabled.description: Check if EKS audit logs are being monitored by GuardDuty (ISO27001 A.8.15 Logging)
aws.guardduty.eks_audit_log_enabled.notes: Specific EKS audit log integration - not covered by detector-level checks. May need EKS service check instead

aws.guardduty.no_high_severity_findings.description: Check that GuardDuty has no active high-severity security findings
aws.guardduty.no_high_severity_findings.notes: This is a posture check (querying findings), not a configuration check. Needs GuardDuty findings API implementation

aws.guardduty.security_center_enabled.description: Memory Protection requirement - possibly GuardDuty runtime protection features
aws.guardduty.security_center_enabled.notes: CCCS SI-16 Memory Protection - could map to basic GuardDuty enabled check, but verify if specific protection features are required

aws.guardduty.vulnerability_assessment_e.notes: GuardDuty doesn't do vulnerability scanning - this might be Amazon Inspector integration or needs clarification on requirement
